name: hecateflow
description: Reusable, MCU-agnostic embedded-development skill system (13 skills) distilled from a real multi-core bare-metal C project. v1.1 adds hardware-mapping headers with single-source polarity/magnitude truth, cross-platform lessons memory, tiered context-saving docs, auto-injection, clangd config management, multi-target IO-ownership planning, and relative-path discipline.

interface.shortDescription: Embedded-dev lifecycle skills: init, design, implement, refactor, review, per-edit safety gate.
interface.longDescription: HecateFlow orchestrates the full embedded-development lifecycle with 13 interactive, MCU-agnostic skills: workspace and per-target initialization, incremental module design, incremental implementation, behavior-preserving refactor, deep review, and an always-on per-edit safety gate (volatile/ISR/numeric/peripheral). v1.1 deepens the engineering discipline: hardware-mapping headers with a single-source polarity/magnitude truth (three direction-coefficient groups, open-loop identification, never hide polarity in a PID Kp sign), a cross-platform lessons memory under .hecateflow/lessons/ (record/recall/avoid/promote so the same mistake is not repeated), tiered context-saving docs, auto-injection so rules and skills are auto-discovered across CLIs, clangd config management, multi-target IO-ownership planning, and relative-path discipline. A persistent project manifest (.hecateflow/project.json) remembers your MCU family, toolchain, build system, targets, scenario constraints, and owned peripherals so skills only ask for what they don't already know.
defaultPrompt: 初始化这个嵌入式工作区
